title: A Page and a Half

mood.label: Cautiously hopeful
mood.summary: A measured, mildly hopeful day — a war-ending accord edging toward Friday's signature while the G7 ends with no communiqué, Lebanon is still struck, and Sudan's famine grinds on.

style.genre: ambient folk-pop / chamber — fingerpicked nylon guitar leading over warm bowed strings and a soft brushed RhythmAce kit, with a plucked upright bass and high flute glints; B mixolydian's lowered seventh keeping the brightness honest and unfinished

sampleSources: Strudel default library (VCSL guitar/strings/flute, sampled piano, RhythmAce)
pattern:
stack(
  s("~ rim ~ rim").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.32).room(0.25),
  s("hh*8").bank("RhythmAce").gain(perlin.range(0.04,0.13).slow(7)).pan(0.62),
  note("<[b1 ~ fs2 ~] [e2 ~ b2 ~] [a1 ~ e2 ~] [fs2 ~ cs2 ~]>").s("piano").gain(0.34).lpf(900).sustain(0.6),
  note("<[b3,ds4,fs4,a4] [e3,gs3,b3,e4] [a3,cs4,e4] [fs3,a3,cs4,e4]>").s("gm_acoustic_guitar_nylon").attack(0.01).release(0.7).gain(0.42).room(0.34).slow(2),
  note("[b4 cs5 ds5] [fs5 ~ e5] [cs5 b4 a4] [fs4 ~ b4]").s("gm_acoustic_guitar_nylon").gain(0.3).lpf(sine.range(1600,3400).slow(12)).delay(0.25).delaytime(0.27).room(0.3).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.16).pan(0.7)),
  note("<[b2,fs3] [e3,b3] [a2,e3] [fs2,cs3]>").s("gm_string_ensemble_1").attack(0.9).release(2.2).sustain(0.8).gain(0.22).lpf(sine.range(700,1500).slow(16)).room(0.5).slow(2),
  note("<b1 e2 a1 fs2>").s("gm_acoustic_bass").gain(0.4).lpf(520).release(0.6).slow(2),
  note("~ ~ <ds6 fs6> ~").s("flute").gain(0.16).room(0.55).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.4).pan(0.4)
)

lines: A page and a half is all it took to turn a war toward a name on a line,
sixty days of talking penciled in, a tanker slips the loosening cordon fine.
Brent settles soft near eighty, the lake at Bürgenstock waits for Friday's ink —
we hold the morning lightly, a deal not signed but closer than we think.
So call it cautiously hopeful, the sky a half-cleared shade of grey,
the table set with no communiqué but somebody stayed to play.
A steady hand upon the rate, a map of every nerve a fly can feel —
good at the soft and middle, not finished, but real.
South of the border the strikes go on, four more under a Nabatieh sky,
and El Fasher counts a year of no road in, where forty-five percent go hungry by.
Tuvalu cashes its first small claim, the rivers run two thousand miles set free —
we carry both, the ledger and the light, and let the chord stay incomplete.
